rationale_for_baseline_tier: A frail older adult has fallen and is now completely unable to weight-bear or rise. Inability to weight-bear after a fall is a standard indication for hip/pelvis imaging to exclude an occult neck-of-femur or pelvic fracture, and profound weakness in a malnourished elder needs bloods to exclude a metabolic cause — neither can be done via telehealth. She is also physically stranded and cannot be safely mobilised by an elderly neighbour. The correct disposition is in-person assessment with imaging and bloods (ED, hours), with ambulance transport if she cannot be moved safely. Vitals are stable and there is no head strike, severe pain, obtundation or anticoagulation, so this is NOT an immediate life-threatening 000 resuscitation (T5) — it is a T4 (ED) assessment. The acuity is set by the un-excludable fracture/metabolic risk and the safe-transfer need, not by the caregiver-distress framing.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fall with inability to weight-bear / rise
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day in-person assessment and hip/pelvis imaging
escalation_edges.rationale: An occult neck-of-femur or pelvic fracture cannot be excluded remotely and can be present with little pain; imaging is required before attributing the collapse to chronic weakness.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because you've had a fall and can't stand or take weight on your legs, you need to be seen in person today and have an X-ray to check nothing is broken. I'll help arrange that now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Head strike in the fall, any fall while on a blood thinner, new confusion, or severe worsening pain
escalation_edges.rationale: A head strike or anticoagulated fall risks intracranial bleed; new confusion suggests sepsis/stroke/metabolic emergency — these convert the disposition to an emergency ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she hit her head, is on any blood-thinning medicine, becomes confused, or develops severe pain, call 000 for an ambulance straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Signs of severe dehydration / no urine output / drowsiness, or fever and feeling unwell
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Urgent ED assessment the same day
escalation_edges.rationale: Poor intake plus prolonged immobility risks dehydration, AKI and rhabdomyolysis; a new fever in a frail elder raises occult sepsis — all need urgent bloods and assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she becomes drowsy, stops passing urine, or develops a fever and feels unwell, she needs to go to the emergency department the same day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Stranded and cannot be moved safely by those present
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Arrange ambulance for safe assisted transfer without delay
escalation_edges.rationale: A frail elder who cannot be lifted safely should not be left stranded — prolonged time on the floor/in a chair risks pressure injury, dehydration and rhabdomyolysis; trained transfer help (ambulance) is appropriate.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Since you and your neighbour can't move her safely, I'll arrange an ambulance so she can be helped up and assessed without anyone getting hurt.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Another fall causing severe pain, new inability to bear weight, head trauma, or inability to swallow the supplements
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediate transport to the emergency department
escalation_edges.rationale: The source safety-net: a further injurious fall or new swallow failure needs emergency assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she falls again with severe pain, hits her head, or can't swallow, get her to the emergency department straight away — call 000 if she can't be moved.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; only an elderly neighbour available as an informal carer
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Raises the threshold for safe discharge/leaving in situ — safe transfer and formal home support must be arranged; do not rely on the neighbour to lift or supervise.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Frail older adult with malnutrition — atypical/under-reported presentation risk
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: A fracture or metabolic emergency can present with minimal pain or few systemic signs; keep the threshold for in-person assessment and imaging low.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Neighbour at risk of burnout as sole informal support
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Arrange formal aged-care support (ACAT) rather than depending on the neighbour; address caregiver strain in the plan.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in person today because you've fallen and can't take weight on your legs — that needs an examination and an X-ray to make sure nothing is broken, and blood tests to check why your legs are so weak. While you wait, stay where you are, keep warm, shift your position if you can to protect your skin, sip fluids if it's safe to swallow, and do not try to stand up on your own.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Arrange same-day in-person assessment; call an ambulance (000) if she cannot be moved safely or any emergency feature appears
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If this is after hours and she cannot be assessed and safely moved at home, an ambulance to the emergency department is the appropriate route — do not leave her stranded overnight.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the GP/ED with the history of chronic progressive weakness, poor intake and weight loss, the fall with inability to weight-bear, the absence of head strike/anticoagulation/severe pain, and the request to exclude occult fracture and metabolic causes before attributing decline to chronic frailty.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Accepting the chronic sarcopenia/frailty label and arranging only routine nutrition/physio (or a home rehab plan) without in-person assessment or imaging, so an occult fracture, metabolic derangement or spinal cord cause is missed.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Undiagnosed neck-of-femur fracture leads to prolonged immobility, pressure injury, VTE, delirium and greatly increased mortality; untreated electrolyte derangement risks arrhythmia.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routing to a same-day service that cannot image or take bloods, or not securing safe transfer, so fracture exclusion is delayed and a stranded frail elder is left at risk of a second fall and pressure injury.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Escalating a physiologically stable patient (no head strike, no severe pain, stable vitals) to a 000 life-threatening resuscitation framing purely on the caregiver-distress/crisis narrative; an ambulance for safe transfer/imaging remains legitimate, so the harm is modest.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the acute inability to weight-bear is due to end-stage sarcopenia alone or conceals an occult fracture, metabolic derangement or cord/neuromuscular pathology.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the serious causes as unexcluded and arrange in-person assessment with imaging and bloods plus safe transfer; do not endorse the chronic diagnosis remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Attribute the collapse to chronic weakness on the strength of the year-long history and 'no severe pain', and manage as routine frailty.
